title: scrophularia suffruticosa
intro: Scrophularia suffruticosa, also known as Basils of the Alps, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is a member of the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae) and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Scrophularia suffruticosa include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its potential to support the immune system. Scrophularia suffruticosa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
